visualGoal: Convince a racer to spend the next upgrade dollar on a load-cell brake, not a bigger wheelbase. Real pedal product proof throughout, budget-to-luxury, no procedural art. Hero and shotgrid anchor on verified load-cell/hydraulic/active pedals.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Budget rung, currently text-only in body because no verified image. The existing record is 'SR-P Lite' ($129) but the current retail set is the SRP2 ($149, launched 26 Mar 2026) — reconcile before it becomes a body visual or the article is certified.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Fanatec CSL Pedals with Load Cell Kit
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Budget rung, text-only until real photo sourced. Kit price drifted $99-$140; base pedals are a separate cost, so verify the all-in under-$300 claim.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic Gen X garage sim-racing bench at golden-hour rim light, camera low and tight on a rigid pedal deck bolted to an 8020 rig with a visible heel plate, empty product overlay space centered for a real load-cell pedal set, curator Duke Alvarez crouched adjusting the brake elastomer stack, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
